plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-312 RP09.P06.M06.S04-RP09.P07.M03.S18 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-312 implements descriptor-only coverage that closes the RP09.P06 permission phase (M06 fixture tail, M07-M09 full permission cycles, M10 closeout head) and opens the RP09.P07 failure foundation (M00-M03: failure taxonomies, missing tenant/actor/matter/resource failures, unknown action/cross-tenant/permission-denied/ambiguous-rule/stale-reference/lock-conflict/retry-exhaustion failures, rollback and compensation expectations, blocked-claim receipts, failure fixtures/tests, integration smokes, audit failure hints, Hermes failure evidence) across nine sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H09), Claude review packet (C09), and CP00-313 handoff, without opening rollback/retry runtime, lock acquisition, runtime permission evaluation, audit event writes, AI runtime dispatch, test runtime paths, state writes, object storage, command runtime execution, Hermes runtime receipts, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-312 is a well-formed descriptor-only CRM-core closeout pack covering exactly 150 RP09 units (RP09.P06.M06.S04 through RP09.P07.M03.S18), closing the RP09.P06 permission phase tail and opening the RP09.P07 failure foundation. Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack-level claim is appropriately conservative: manifest and command-evidence report production_ready:false / implemented_pending_review with no premature enterprise-trust claim. Coverage is enforced in code: CRM_CORE_CP312_REQUIREMENTS distributions (deliverable, phase, micro-phase, micro-title) match the prompt baseline, the live closeout-pack-plan.json pack, the manifest plan_binding_snapshot, and the review baseline; validateCrmCoreCp312Coverage checks every count, unit-id ordering, RP09-only scope, and per-section row titles against the live plan, and model.test.js asserts validity (node --test exit 0). No-write/no-real-data boundaries are comprehensive and enforced: freezeCp312Result and CRM_CORE_CP312_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION pin all runtime, DB, object-storage, audit-event, Hermes-receipt, lock, retry, rollback, AI-dispatch, and test-runtime flags to false with synthetic_only/no_real_data true, and the descriptor validator rejects any drift plus per-row leak guards (permission-decision, audit-hint, blocked-claim, lock-token, fixture-payload, cross-tenant). The authority boundary holds end to end (registry constant, service descriptor authority_boundary, contract projection, validator assertions, test): Claude is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob), not final approver; local validation does not claim enterprise trust; human approval is required before any downstream runtime opening (CP00-313). Hermes is used only as a deterministic descriptor-only review packet/receipt (receipt_shape descriptor_only…no_write, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), not embedded in product runtime. CP178+ review hardening is present via forbidden_review_evidence (failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, disabled_tools_run, missing_raw_output_reference, missing_source_inspection_basis, claude_final_approval_claim, source_mutation_claim, production_enterprise_trust_claim), and the readiness auth-preflight returned valid JSON (1436 bytes, exit 0). Changed-file-scope correctly isolates the eight pack files and preserves unrelated dirty files without attributing them to the pack. No P0–P3 findings; the pack is consistent across registry, service, validators, contract, tests, manifest, plan, README, and index re-exports.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-312 crm core p06 closeout p07 failure foundation descriptor pack
